entries.text_ar: O you who believe! Do not be forward in the presence of God and His Messengerï؟½He said:Verily, God, Exalted is He, has instructed His believing servants in propriety (adab). The meaning is: ï؟½Do not speak before he [the Prophet] speaksï؟½. Then when he speaks, move forward towards him, hearkening and listening to him; ï؟½and fear God, regarding the neglect of His rights, and the loss of reverence for Him. Surely God hears what you say, and knows what you do.His words:

ï؟½Shun much suspicionï؟½He said:That is, ï؟½Do not discredit anyone [by holding] a bad opinion (sإ«ت¾ al-ل؛“ann) about them without [knowing] the truth (ل¸¥aqؤ«qa) [of the matter].ï؟½ Indeed the Prophet said: ï؟½The most untruthful of reports is that of opinion (ل؛“ann).ï؟½ Then Sahl said:Bad opinion comes from ignorance and pertains to the natural self (nafs al-ل¹­abت؟). The most ignorant person is the one who estranges his heart [from God] without being aware of it. Indeed, God, Exalted is He, has said: And that suspicion of yours which you held about your Lord has ruined you, so you have become among the losers. [41:23] Certainly, the servant is deprived of blessed provision and prayer at night because of bad opinion.One night a man, one of [Godï؟½s] servants, slept through [and missed] his customary rite (wird) and felt regret over it. [He] was asked: ï؟½Do you feel regret about [missing] that which you are wont to perform?ï؟½ He replied, ï؟½I do not feel regret because of that, but rather because of the sin through which I became deprived of that good [deed].ï؟½Sahl was asked, ï؟½What is the meaning of the Prophetï؟½s words : ï؟½Be on your guard with people, [by holding a] bad opinion (sإ«ت¾ al-ل؛“ann).ï؟½ï؟½ He replied:The meaning of this is [that protection from people] is [gained by holding a] bad opinion of yourself, not of other people. In other words, accuse your own self for not treating them fairly in your dealings with them. His words:ï؟½and do not spyï؟½He said:That is, ï؟½Do not search out the faults that God has covered for His servants, for you may well be afflicted by that [fault].It was related of Jesus that he used to say, ï؟½Do not speak too much other than in remembranceof God, Mighty and Majestic is He, for your hearts will be hardened, and the heart that is hard is far from God. Do not regard the faults of people as if you were their masters, but look at your own works as if they were your slaves.

ï؟½Say, ï؟½You do not have faith; rather say, ï؟½We have submittedï؟½ï؟½ï؟½ He said:This means [they should admit], ï؟½We [only] affirmed [the faith] from fear of being taken captive or killed; for faith (ؤ«mؤپn) is in the truthful affirmation of the tongue (iqrؤپr al-lisؤپn ل¹£idqan), and certainty within the heart as a binding pact [with God] (ؤ«qؤپn fؤ«ï؟½l-qalb ت؟aqdan), along with the realisation (taل¸¥qؤ«q) of these through the bodily members with sincerity (ikhlؤپل¹£). Faith (ؤ«mؤپn) has nothing to do with affiliations (ansؤپb); rather affiliations are only a part of Islam. The Muslim is beloved of people but the believer is in no need (ghanؤ«) of people. His words:

They deem it to be a favour to you that they have submittedï؟½Namely, that they affirmed as true that to which you were summoning them. (Say) ï؟½ï؟½Rather it is God who has done you a favour in that He has guided you to faith, if you are being truthful,ï؟½that is, if you are aware that it is God who blessed you with guidance from the beginning.Sahl said:I practised scrupulous piety (waraت؟) for forty years and it happened that [my] attention was turned from me â¸¢towards Himâ¸£ and He corrected me with His words: They deem it to be a favour to you that they have submitted. But God Glorified and Exalted is He knows best.His words, Exalted is He:

ï؟½and do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophetï؟½That is, ï؟½Do not address him except when seeking to understand [from him].ï؟½ Then He explains the honour of the one who reveres Him, and says:
ï؟½they are the ones whose hearts God has tested for mindfulness of Himï؟½That is, He has made their intentions pure for Him.His words:

ï؟½If a reprobate comes to you with some tidingï؟½He said:The reprobate (fؤپsiq) is a liar (kadhdhؤپb). The inner meaning of the verse is to teach (taت¾dؤ«b) a person who has been informed of someoneï؟½s derogation of him, that he should not rush into exacting retribution on them, without having found out about [the matter] for himself.His words:

ï؟½God had endeared faith to you and made it beautiful to your heartsï؟½He said:As a kindness (ت؟aل¹­f) from Him, He singled out your hearts for His worship and endowed them with sincerity. [This is so] because being singled out (istikhlؤپل¹£) is from His kindness (ت؟aل¹­f), while [your] sincerity (ikhlؤپل¹£) is His right (ل¸¥aqq). A servant cannot fulfil His right save through His kindness in granting him assistance through the means of faith (asbؤپb al-ؤ«mؤپn), which are the irrefutable proofs and wondrous signs (ؤپyؤپt muت؟jiza).His words:ï؟½He has made disbelief, mischief and disobedience hateful to youï؟½due to the fear of His abhorrent punishment.His words:

[that is] a favour from God and a blessingï؟½He said:God has favoured them by that which He initially granted them, and He has guided them to Him with various kinds of proximity (qurb) and intimacy (zulf).His words:

If two parties of believers fall to fighting, you [believers] make peace between themï؟½He said:The outward meaning of the verse is as those specialised in exegesis have explained. However, in its inner meaning it refers to the spirit (rإ«ل¸¥), intellect (ت؟aql), heart (qalb), basic nature (ل¹­abت؟), desire (hawؤپ) and lust (shahwa). If natural instinct, desire and lust take up arms against the heart, intellect and spirit, the servant must fight them with the swords of vigilance (murؤپqaba), the arrows of inspection (muل¹­ؤپlaت؟a) and the lights of conformity (muwؤپfaqa), so that the spirit and the intellect gain the upper hand, and desire and lust are vanquished.His words:
